name: Low blood sugar (hypo)
summary: Hypoglycaemia. Common in diabetic children but can happen to anyone — missed meal, over-exertion, illness.
recognition: Strange, uncharacteristic behaviour — can look like drunkenness

callEmergency.text: Call 999 if their condition does not improve with sugar, or if they become unconscious.

steps: Give a sugary drink, sugar lumps, sweet food, or glucose tablets (if they carry them).
If they have their own glucose gel — help them use it.
If they recover quickly — give more food/drink (ideally fast-acting carbs).
Let them rest until they feel better.
Help them find their glucose testing kit if they have one.
Monitor level of response, breathing, pulse.
If they become unconscious — open airway, check breathing.

warnings.text: Don't give food or drink to someone whose consciousness is impaired — they could choke.
